metadata.title: High School — Functions
metadata.notes: Auto-ingested from Common Standards Project (set 67810E9EF6944F9383DCC602A3484C23_D10003FB_high-school-functions). Node structure matches KnowledgeNode in knowledge_zome/integrity/src/lib.rs.

nodes.title: (+) Prove the addition and subtraction formulas for sine, cosine, and tangent...
nodes.description: (+) Prove the addition and subtraction formulas for sine, cosine, and tangent and use them to solve problems.

academic_standards.description: (+) Prove the addition and subtraction formulas for sine, cosine, and tangent and use them to solve problems.

nodes.title: Prove the Pythagorean identity sin²(θ) + cos²(θ) = 1 and use it to find s...
nodes.description: Prove the Pythagorean identity sin²(θ) + cos²(θ) = 1 and use it to find sin(θ), cos(θ), or tan(θ) given sin(θ), cos(θ), or tan(θ) and the quadrant of the angle.

academic_standards.description: Prove the Pythagorean identity sin²(θ) + cos²(θ) = 1 and use it to find sin(θ), cos(θ), or tan(θ) given sin(θ), cos(θ), or tan(θ) and the quadrant of the angle.

nodes.title: (+) Use inverse functions to solve trigonometric equations that arise in mode...
nodes.description: (+) Use inverse functions to solve trigonometric equations that arise in modeling contexts; evaluate the solutions using technology, and interpret them in terms of the context.

academic_standards.description: (+) Use inverse functions to solve trigonometric equations that arise in modeling contexts; evaluate the solutions using technology, and interpret them in terms of the context.

nodes.title: (+) Understand that restricting a trigonometric function to a domain on which...
nodes.description: (+) Understand that restricting a trigonometric function to a domain on which it is always increasing or always decreasing allows its inverse to be constructed.

academic_standards.description: (+) Understand that restricting a trigonometric function to a domain on which it is always increasing or always decreasing allows its inverse to be constructed.

nodes.title: (+) Use special triangles to determine geometrically the values of sine, cosi...
nodes.description: (+) Use special triangles to determine geometrically the values of sine, cosine, tangent for π/3, π/4 and π/6, and use the unit circle to express the values of sine, cosine, and tangent for π-x, π+x, and 2π-x in terms of their values for x, where x is any real number.

academic_standards.description: (+) Use special triangles to determine geometrically the values of sine, cosine, tangent for π/3, π/4 and π/6, and use the unit circle to express the values of sine, cosine, and tangent for π-x, π+x, and 2π-x in terms of their values for x, where x is any real number.

nodes.title: Explain how the unit circle in the coordinate plane enables the extension of ...
nodes.description: Explain how the unit circle in the coordinate plane enables the extension of trigonometric functions to all real numbers, interpreted as radian measures of angles traversed counterclockwise around the unit circle.

academic_standards.description: Explain how the unit circle in the coordinate plane enables the extension of trigonometric functions to all real numbers, interpreted as radian measures of angles traversed counterclockwise around the unit circle.

nodes.title: Understand radian measure of an angle as the length of the arc on the unit ci...
nodes.description: Understand radian measure of an angle as the length of the arc on the unit circle subtended by the angle.

academic_standards.description: Understand radian measure of an angle as the length of the arc on the unit circle subtended by the angle.

nodes.description: For exponential models, express as a logarithm the solution to ab<sup>ct</sup> = d where a, c, and d are numbers and the base b is 2, 10, or e; evaluate the logarithm using technology.

nodes.title: Construct linear and exponential functions, including arithmetic and geometri...

nodes.title: Identify the effect on the graph of replacing f(x) by f(x) + k, k f(x), f(kx)...
nodes.description: Identify the effect on the graph of replacing f(x) by f(x) + k, k f(x), f(kx), and f(x + k) for specific values of k (both positive and negative); find the value of k given the graphs. Experiment with cases and illustrate an explanation of the effects on the graph using technology. Include recognizing even and odd functions from their graphs and algebraic expressions for them.

academic_standards.description: Identify the effect on the graph of replacing f(x) by f(x) + k, k f(x), f(kx), and f(x + k) for specific values of k (both positive and negative); find the value of k given the graphs. Experiment with cases and illustrate an explanation of the effects on the graph using technology. Include recognizing even and odd functions from their graphs and algebraic expressions for them.

nodes.title: Use function notation, evaluate functions for inputs in their domains, and in...

nodes.title: Understand that a function from one set (called the domain) to another set (c...
nodes.description: Understand that a function from one set (called the domain) to another set (called the range) assigns to each element of the domain exactly one element of the range. If f is a function and x is an element of its domain, then f(x) denotes the output of f corresponding to the input x. The graph of f is the graph of the equation y = f(x).

academic_standards.description: Understand that a function from one set (called the domain) to another set (called the range) assigns to each element of the domain exactly one element of the range. If f is a function and x is an element of its domain, then f(x) denotes the output of f corresponding to the input x. The graph of f is the graph of the equation y = f(x).
